feat: rendering optimisation pipeline (#1306, #1307, #1308, #1309) (#1310)

* feat: add render instrumentation and strategy comparison

Add RenderInstrumentation class to capture per-render metrics:
- Total render time (Stopwatch-based)
- PipeWriter GetSpan/FlushAsync call counts
- Fragment copy count
- Allocation delta via GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread

Add InstrumentedPipeWriter wrapper that transparently counts
PipeWriter operations without adding hot-path allocations.

Add RenderBenchmark utility for single-shot and averaged
multi-iteration benchmarking of render strategies.

Add docs/render-strategy-comparison.md documenting 4 evaluated
strategies (UTF-8 Fragment, Arena, Compiled, Template-with-holes)
with recommendation for Strategy D.

9 new tests covering all instrumentation counters, snapshot,
reset, data integrity, and benchmark averaging.

Closes #1306

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: template-with-holes render architecture

Implement precompiled template execution pipeline:

- RenderSegment struct: static UTF-8 byte fragments or dynamic
  field index placeholders (holes) with raw-HTML flag
- RenderPlan: flat array of segments with field key mapping and
  precomputed static byte count for size estimation
- TemplatePlanCompiler: parses template strings at startup,
  pre-encodes static text to UTF-8 bytes, deduplicates field
  indices for repeated keys, handles malformed tokens gracefully
- TemplatePlanExecutor: linear segment iteration with page-key
  precedence over app-key resolution, direct PipeWriter output,
  HTML encoding for safety (html_ prefix bypasses encoding)

Zero allocations in the render hot path — all compilation
happens at startup, execution is sequential copy + format.

26 new tests covering compilation, execution, key precedence,
HTML encoding, raw HTML, repeated keys, UTF-8, and scale.

Closes #1307

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: SIMD-accelerated fragment copy for renderer

Add SimdCopy.CopyFragment using Vector128 (portable across x64
SSE2 and ARM64 NEON/AdvSIMD) for fragments >= 16 bytes:
- Main loop processes 16 bytes per iteration via vector
  load/store
- Tail bytes handled with Unsafe.CopyBlockUnaligned
- Fragments < 16 bytes fall back to Span.CopyTo

Integrate SIMD copy into TemplatePlanExecutor.WriteFragment
so all static HTML fragments in compiled render plans use
vectorised memory operations.

11 new tests covering: empty, 1-byte, 15-byte (fallback),
16-byte (exact vector), 17-byte (vector + tail), 20-byte,
32-byte, 256-byte, typical HTML fragment size, and end-to-end
PipeWriter integration.

Closes #1308

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: single-span render pipeline for minimal PipeWriter overhead

Add SingleSpanRenderer that precomputes response size and renders
the entire output into a single contiguous buffer:
- EstimateTotalSize: sums static fragment sizes + dynamic value
  estimates with HTML entity expansion headroom
- RenderToSpan: writes all segments into a pre-allocated Span<byte>
  using SIMD fragment copy + inline HTML encoding, tracking offset
  with a single integer
- RenderAsync: single GetSpan(totalSize) → render → single
  Advance(bytesWritten) → single FlushAsync

Verified via InstrumentedPipeWriter: exactly 1 GetSpan, 1 Advance,
1 FlushAsync per render — compared to O(segments) calls in the
multi-write TemplatePlanExecutor.

15 new tests covering substitution, key precedence, HTML encoding,
raw HTML, UTF-8, size estimation, span rendering, instrumented
single-flush verification, and comparison with multi-write renderer.

Closes #1309

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: pre-build Flutter in CI, map grouping, cursor pagination & lazy city profiles (#94)

## Summary

Three improvements rolled into one branch:

### 1. Pre-built Flutter CI + slim Docker image
- Flutter is built in CI via `subosito/flutter-action`, artifact is
passed to Docker
- Docker image reduced to nginx-only (`nginx:stable-alpine`) — no
Flutter SDK at runtime
- `nginx.conf` added: `Cache-Control: no-store` on `index.html` /
`flutter_service_worker.js`, SPA fallback for deep links
- `entrypoint.sh` injects `API_BASE_URL` at container start via `sed` so
the image is environment-agnostic

### 2. Startup crash fix
- PR #91 removed `DbManager` from providers but `AppLoadingPage` still
called `context.read<DbManager>()` → blank screen on launch
- Removed stale `DbManager` dependency from `AppLoadingManager` and
`AppLoadingPage`

### 3. Map location grouping, cursor pagination & lazy city profiles
- **`MapGateway` / `MapGatewayImpl`** — new gateway calling `GET
/profile/map`; returns alumni pre-grouped by city with coordinates

commit.message:
Fix Grid column title not visible when column is in a group (#690)

In paintColumnHeaderWithGroup(), the 'width' variable was used for both
the accumulated group width and the individual column width. After the
while loop accumulated widths of all columns in the group, the inflated
value was incorrectly passed to the column header renderer's setBounds(),
causing right-aligned column titles and sort arrows to not be visible.

Introduce a separate 'groupWidth' variable for the group header width
accumulation, keeping 'width' as the individual column width.

- Plugin specs define host-specific extension/runtime hooks.
- Agent Skills define portable instruction packages that can be reused across agent systems.

feat(frontend): Give priority to token list in OpenCryptoPay (#11862)

# Motivation

When paying, tokens were sorted by USD value descending, but the top
options often had very close or identical displayed fiat values. This
could result in ICP tokens (which use the cheaper ICRC-2 flow) appearing
after EVM or BTC tokens, even though they would be the better default
choice for the user.

# Changes

- Updated the token comparator in `open-crypto-pay.store.ts` to sort by
displayed fiat value **ascending** (cheapest option first).
- Added secondary sort criteria when fiat values are equal: ICP network
first, then EVM networks, then BTC.
- Added tertiary/quaternary sorts by token symbol and network name (both
ascending) for stable, deterministic ordering.
- The default selected token in the pay form naturally picks the first
sorted entry, so the cheapest ICP option is now preferred.

# Tests

Added tests.
